content: Socket.io v4 Connection State Recovery: server temporarily retains a disconnected socket's id, rooms, and data for a configurable maxDisconnectionDuration; on reconnect within that window the client sends session id + last offset and the server restores rooms/data, with socket.recovered flag indicating success. skipMiddlewares option controls whether auth middleware reruns. Useful for our F5-recovery requirement but has limits (fails after long disconnect, server restart, or unsupported adapter) — safer to implement our own explicit session-token-in-localStorage + server-side team-state map rather than relying solely on built-in recovery, since a mid-class Node restart would wipe socket.io's recovery buffer anyway (state lives in our own in-memory game object, not just socket internals).
